title: [FIRRTL][CreateSiFiveMetadata] Check if the use of `SignalMappingAnnotation` is correct
body:
`CreateSiFiveMetadata` will not emit information about modules annotated with `SignalMappingAnnotation`.  https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/2e87750c597abe0a12c5b94cb00f441159203e17/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/Transforms/CreateSiFiveMetadata.cpp#L332
This might have been copied from the original pass implementation. 
This annotation might not ever actually appear in the annotation file, instead it is the parent of two other annotations.  This issue is to check which if the logic here is working as intended.
The original annotations are closed SiFive source.

```scala
/** Maps a signal in a sub-circuit to a signal in the design circuit
  */
trait SignalMappingAnnotation extends Annotation {
  def targetDirection: Direction
  def designRef:       ReferenceTarget
  def target:          ReferenceTarget
}

case class SourceAnnotation(target: ReferenceTarget, designRef: ReferenceTarget)
    extends SignalMappingAnnotation
    with HasGeneratedExtModules
    with HasDontTouches
    with SingleTargetAnnotation[ReferenceTarget] 

case class SinkAnnotation(target: ReferenceTarget, designRef: ReferenceTarget, cond: Option[CondReferenceTarget])
    extends SignalMappingAnnotation
    with HasGeneratedExtModules
    with HasDontTouches
```
